2605.29240.title: Surfacing Isolated Learners with Outcome-Independent Mediation of Feedback between Teachers and Students Using AI
2605.29240.paper_summary: AI-augmented classrooms generate rich teacher and student feedback before graded outcomes become available, yet these signals can be difficult to translate into timely instructional decisions. We propose an interpretable decision layer: a transparent mechanism that ranks course topics requiring attention without using grades or post-hoc outcome labels. The approach combines three signals: student learning difficulty prevalence, disagreement between learner self-reports and observed difficulties, and unresolved teacher concerns. The output is a ranked set of topic priorities with per-topic decision records explaining each ranking. In one graduate CS course offering ($n=5$ instructor interviews; $n=279$ survey responses), prioritized topics aligned with instructor concerns (top-5 overlap 3/5; Spearman $ρ=0.80$) and student-reported topic difficulty ($ρ=0.46$, $p=.048$). Multi-signal integration also surfaced learners not identified through individual signal sources alone (AUC $=0.96$ vs. $0.91$ for gap prevalence alone). Reflective thinking, help-seeking, and self-efficacy provided additional evidence that student behavioral signals align with learning-related constructs. While preliminary, these findings suggest that transparent coordination mechanisms may help support human-AI co-agency when feedback is incomplete.
2605.29240.paper_summary_zh: AI增強的課堂在成績結果可用之前產生豐富的教師和學生反饋，但這些信號可能難以轉化為及時的教學決策。我們提出了一個可解釋的決策層：一種透明的機制，對需要注意的課程主題進行排名，而不使用成績或事後結果標籤。這種方法結合了三個信號：學生學習困難的普遍性、學習者自我報告與觀察到的困難之間的分歧，以及未解決的教師擔憂。輸出是一組按優先順序排列的主題優先級，並附有每個主題的決策記錄，解釋每個排名。在一個研究生計算機科學課程中（$n=5$ 教師訪談；$n=279$ 調查回應），優先考慮的主題與教師的擔憂一致（前五名重疊3/5；Spearman $ρ=0.80$）以及學生報告的主題難度（$ρ=0.46$, $p=.048$）。多信號整合還揭示了通過單一信號來源無法識別的學習者（AUC $=0.96$ 與僅考慮差距普遍性時的 $0.91$）。反思性思維、尋求幫助和自我效能提供了額外的證據，表明學生行為信號與學習相關的構念一致。儘管這些發現仍屬初步，但它們表明透明的協調機制可能有助於在反饋不完整時支持人類與AI的共同代理。
2605.29240.author: Junsoo Park et.al.
2605.29240.authors: Junsoo Park, Youssef Medhat, Htet Phyo Wai, Ploy Thajchayapong, Ashok K. Goel
